gaussian_basis.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Basis Sets ## Overview Most methods require a basis set specification. If no basis set keyword is included, STO-3G is used by default. Exceptions include:

gaussian_cc.md.name: gaussian_cc.md
gaussian_cc.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Coupled Cluster Methods [Bartlett78], using double substitutions from the Hartree-Fock determinant for CCD [Pople78], or both single and double substitutions

gaussian_dft.md.name: gaussian_dft.md
gaussian_dft.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 DFT Methods Density Functional (DFT) Methods * Description

gaussian_freq.md.name: gaussian_freq.md
gaussian_freq.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Frequency Calculations Stable keyword to test the stability of Hartree-Fock and DFT wavefunctions.

gaussian_hf.md.name: gaussian_hf.md
gaussian_hf.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Hartree-Fock Method This method keyword requests a Hartree-Fock calculation [Roothaan51]. Unless explicitly specified, RHF is used for singlets and UHF for

gaussian_input.md.name: gaussian_input.md
gaussian_input.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Input ## Overview Gaussian 16 input consists of a series of lines in an ASCII text file. The basic structure includes several sections:

gaussian_irc.md.name: gaussian_irc.md
gaussian_irc.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 IRC (Intrinsic Reaction Coordinate) ## Overview The `IRC` keyword requests that a reaction path be followed by integrating the intrinsic reaction coordinate. The initial geometry is the transition state, and the path can be followed in one or both directions.

gaussian_mp.md.name: gaussian_mp.md
gaussian_mp.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Møller-Plesset Perturbation Theory The MPn method keywords request a Hartree-Fock calculation (by default, RHF for singlets, UHF for higher multiplicities) followed by a

gaussian_nmr.md.name: gaussian_nmr.md
gaussian_nmr.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 NMR ## Overview The `NMR` keyword predicts NMR shielding tensors and magnetic susceptibilities using HF, DFT, or MP2 methods.

gaussian_oniom.md.name: gaussian_oniom.md
gaussian_oniom.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 ONIOM ## Overview The `ONIOM` keyword requests a two- or three-layer ONIOM calculation. The molecular system is divided into layers treated with different model chemistries, and results are automatically combined.

gaussian_opt.md.name: gaussian_opt.md
gaussian_opt.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Geometry Optimization Hartree-Fock, CIS, MP2, MP3, MP4(SDQ), CID, CISD, CCD, CCSD, QCISD, BD, CASSCF, and all DFT and semi-empirical methods, the default algorithm

gaussian_output.md.name: gaussian_output.md
gaussian_output.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Output ## Overview The `Output` keyword creates input files for external programs or writes Fortran unformatted files containing calculation results. Its options control the contents of the created file.

gaussian_parallel.md.name: gaussian_parallel.md
gaussian_parallel.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Parallel Computing ## Overview Gaussian 16 supports shared-memory parallelism on multi-core systems. Parallel execution is controlled through Link 0 commands that specify processor count, memory allocation, and scratch file management.

gaussian_pop.md.name: gaussian_pop.md
gaussian_pop.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Population Analysis (Pop) ## Overview Population analysis methods partition the electron density among atoms to determine atomic charges, bond orders, and orbital character. The `Pop` keyword controls which population analysis is performed.

gaussian_scf.md.name: gaussian_scf.md
gaussian_scf.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 SCF Keyword ## Overview The `SCF` keyword controls the Self-Consistent Field procedure behavior. In Gaussian 16, `SCF=Tight` is the default.

gaussian_solvation.md.name: gaussian_solvation.md
gaussian_solvation.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 SCRF (Solvation) ## Overview The `SCRF` keyword requests a calculation in the presence of a solvent by placing the solute in a cavity within the solvent reaction field.

gaussian_td.md.name: gaussian_td.md
gaussian_td.md.summary: # Gaussian 16 Time-Dependent DFT time-dependent Hartree-Fock or DFT method [Bauernschmitt96a, Casida98, Stratmann98, VanCaillie99, VanCaillie00, Furche02, Scalmani06];
